# send

[![NPM Version][npm-image]][npm-url]
[![NPM Downloads][downloads-image]][downloads-url]
[![Linux Build][travis-image]][travis-url]
[![Windows Build][appveyor-image]][appveyor-url]
[![Test Coverage][coveralls-image]][coveralls-url]

Send is a library for streaming static files to a client, with a focus on high performance and security. It's designed to serve files efficiently with proper content types, conditional GET support, and other HTTP features.  

This module is used internally by Express.js for its `res.sendFile()` method and static file serving capabilities.

## Installation

This is a [Node.js](https://nodejs.org/en/) module available through the
[npm registry](https://www.npmjs.com/). Installation is done using the
[`npm install` command](https://docs.npmjs.com/getting-started/installing-npm-packages-locally):

```bash
$ npm install send
```

## API

```js
var send = require('send')
```

### send(req, path, [options])

Create a new `SendStream` for the given path to send to a `res`. The `req` is
the Node.js HTTP request and the `path` is a urlencoded path to send (urlencoded,
not the actual file-system path).

#### Options

##### dotfiles

Set how "dotfiles" are treated when encountered. A dotfile is a file or directory
that begins with a dot ("."). Note this check is done on the path itself without
checking if the path actually exists on the disk. If `root` is specified, only the
dotfiles above the root are checked (i.e. the root itself can be within a dotfile
when when set to "deny").

  - `'allow'` No special treatment for dotfiles
  - `'deny'` Send a 403 for any request for a dotfile
  - `'ignore'` Pretend like the dotfile does not exist and 404

The default value is `'ignore'`.

##### end

Specify if the `res.end()` method should be called automatically. This is `true` by
default.

##### etag

Enable or disable etag generation, defaults to `true`.

##### extensions

Set file extension fallbacks. When set, if a file is not found, the given extensions
will be added to the file name and search for. The first that exists will be served.
Example: `['html', 'htm']`.

The default value is `false`.

##### immutable

Enable or disable the `immutable` directive in the `Cache-Control` response header.
If enabled, the `maxAge` option should also be specified to enable caching. The
`immutable` directive will prevent supported clients from making conditional requests
during the life of the `maxAge` option to check if the file has changed.

##### index

By default send supports "index.html" files, to disable this set `false` or to
supply a new index pass a string or an array in preferred order.

##### lastModified

Enable or disable `Last-Modified` header, defaults to `true`. Uses the file system's
last modified value.

##### maxAge

Provide a max-age in milliseconds for http caching, defaults to `0`. This can also
be a string accepted by the [ms](https://www.npmjs.org/package/ms#readme) module.

##### root

Root directory for relative filenames.

##### start

Specify the position at which to start sending a file. This is inclusive and starts
at `0`.

##### cacheControl

Enable or disable sending the `Cache-Control` header, defaults to `true`.

### Events

The `SendStream` is an event emitter and will emit the following events:

#### .on('error', callback)

Emitted when an error occurs. An `Error` object is provided as the only argument
to the callback.

#### .on('directory', callback)

Emitted when a directory is requested and the "directory" handling feature is
enabled. The directory URL is provided as the only argument to the callback.

#### .on('file', callback)

Emitted when a file is requested. The file path is provided as the only argument
to the callback.

#### .on('headers', callback)

Emitted when the response headers are ready to be sent. The callback is given
the request, response, and a function to get the headers.

#### .on('stream', callback)

Emitted when the file is being streamed. The callback is given the request,
response, and a file stream.

#### .on('end', callback)

Emitted when the response has completed. The callback is given no arguments.

## Examples

### Serving files with vanilla Node.js http server

```js
var http = require('http')
var send = require('send')

var server = http.createServer(function onRequest(req, res) {
  // Assume all requests are for files
  send(req, req.url, {root: '/path/to/static/files'})
    .on('error', function onError(err) {
      res.statusCode = err.status || 500
      res.end(err.message)
    })
    .pipe(res)
})

server.listen(3000)
```

### Serving files with Express.js

Express.js uses this module internally for its `res.sendFile()` method and static file serving.

```js
var express = require('express')
var app = express()

// Serve files from the 'public' directory
app.use(express.static('public'))

// Or use res.sendFile() for specific files
app.get('/file', function (req, res) {
  res.sendFile('/path/to/file.txt')
})

app.listen(3000)
```

## Error Handling

The `send` module provides detailed error objects that include the status code and message:

```js
var send = require('send')

send(req, req.url)
  .on('error', function onError(err) {
    console.error(err.status, err.message)
    // => 404 Not Found
    // => 403 Forbidden
    // etc
  })
```
